title: Injectable fluid control valve
abstract: A fluid flow control valve (10) configured to provide a counter flow of a secondary fluid (22) into a primary fluid flow (23) so as to stabilise or regulate turbulence and pressure changes with a conduit network. The valve (10) may find particular application within oil and gas production systems and to reduce or eliminate slugging in flow lines that use risers for vertical fluid transport.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A valve to effect at least one characteristic of a flow of fluid through a conduit, the valve comprising: a main body having an internal bore provided with a bore inlet and outlet at respective axial ends of the bore to allow a primary fluid flow through the bore; an injector port having a port outlet in fluid communication with the bore and positioned at the main body to allow injection of a secondary fluid flow into the bore between the bore inlet and outlet; wherein at least the port outlet is arranged so as to create a second fluid flow resultant from the injector port orientated generally transverse to the longitudinal axis of the bore and aligned counter to the primary fluid flow. 2. The valve as claimed in claim 1 wherein at least the port outlet is configured to direct the secondary fluid flow in an axial direction within the bore towards the bore inlet relative to the bore outlet. 3. The valve as claimed in claims 1 or 2 wherein the injector port has an internal passageway terminating at the port outlet, a longitudinal axis of the passageway aligned transverse to the longitudinal axis of the bore. 4. The valve as claimed in claim 3 wherein the longitudinal axis of the passageway at or towards the port outlet is aligned at an angle in a range 20 to 60Â° relative to the longitudinal axis of the bore. 5. The valve as claimed in claim 4 wherein the range is 40 to 50Â°. 6. The valve as claimed in any one of claims 3 to 5 wherein a diameter of the passageway is less than a diameter of the bore. 7. The valve as claimed in claim 6 wherein the diameter of the passageway is less than 70%, less than 60%, less than 50%, less than 40%, less than 30% or less than 20% of the diameter of the bore. 8. The valve as claimed in any preceding claim being a venturi valve in which the bore comprises a construction. 9. The valve as claimed in claim 8 wherein the port outlet is positioned in a lengthwise direction of the bore at the construction. 10. The valve as claimed in claim 9 wherein the port outlet is positioned at a smallest diameter region of the construction. 1 1. A valve to effect at least one flow characteristic of a flow of a fluid through a conduit, the valve comprising: a main body having an internal bore provided with a bore inlet and outlet at respective axial ends of the bore to allow a primary fluid flow through the bore; an injector port having a port outlet in fluid communication with the bore and positioned at the main body to allow injection of a secondary fluid flow into the bore between the bore inlet and outlet; wherein at least the port outlet is arranged, in a plane perpendicular to a longitudinal axis of the bore, off-centre relative an axial centre of the bore to create a second fluid flow resultant from the injector port that is directed to a region between the axial centre and an internal surface of the main body that defines the bore so as to induce a rotational flow path of the primary fluid flow around the longitudinal axis of the bore downstream of the valve. 12. The valve as claimed in claim 1 1 wherein in the plane perpendicular to the longitudinal axis of the bore at least the port outlet is arranged such that at least a majority of the second fluid flow is orientated to be off-centre from the axial centre of the bore. 13. The valve as claimed in claim 12 wherein the majority of the second fluid flow is directed into the bore primarily towards the internal surface of the main body that defines the bore. 14. The valve as claimed in any one of claims 1 1 to 13 comprising a single injector port. 15. The valve as claimed in any one of claims 1 1 to 13 comprising a plurality of injector ports, each port configured to provide a respective said second fluid flow and positioned at different regions of the bore. 16. The valve as claimed in claim 15 wherein the plurality of injector ports are positioned in the same plane aligned perpendicular to the longitudinal axis of the bore. 17. The valve as claimed in any one of claims 1 1 to 16 wherein the injector port has an internal passageway terminating at the port outlet, a longitudinal axis of the passageway in a plane perpendicular to the longitudinal axis of the bore being aligned tangential or at a secant to the internal surface that defines the bore. 18. Fluid flow apparatus to provide transport of a fluid comprising: a conduit network connecting a fluid source to a fluid delivery location, the network capable of providing at least a primary fluid flow from the source to the delivery location; a valve as claimed in any preceding claim located within the network at a position of the primary fluid flow between the source and the delivery location. 19. The apparatus as claimed in claim 18 wherein the network comprises a riser to provide transport of the fluid against gravity in the upward direction from the source at a first height position to the delivery location at a second height position separated from the source. 20. The apparatus as claimed in claim 19 wherein the valve is positioned in the direction of the primary fluid flow downstream of the riser. 21. The apparatus as claimed in claim 19 wherein the valve is positioned in a height direction closer to the delivery location than the source. 22. The apparatus as claimed in any one of claims 17 to 21 further comprising a choke valve component positioned downstream of the valve. 23. The apparatus as claimed in any one of claims 17 to 22 further comprising a control system having a plurality of sensors positioned at the conduit network, the control system configured to control at least one of a flow rate, a volume of fluid, a velocity of fluid, a pressure of the secondary fluid flow delivered by the injector port into the bore.
